title: Diplomat
description: Diplomats represent India's interests abroad, fostering international relations, negotiating treaties, and protecting Indian citizens in foreign countries. They work to promote peace, trade, and cultural exchange.
content: Diplomats require excellent communication, negotiation, and cross-cultural skills. They often begin their careers by passing the Civil Services Examination (CSE) conducted by the UPSC and joining the Indian Foreign Service (IFS). The work involves extensive travel, representing India in international forums, and advocating for national policies.

financialFeasibility.note: While the UPSC exam is accessible to all, coaching and higher education, especially in international relations or related fields, can be expensive. Scholarships and government support are available.

sectorScore.remarks: Diplomacy is almost exclusively a government sector career. Opportunities in related NGOs or international organizations exist but are significantly fewer.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares similar skills in policy implementation and public service.
relatedCareers.title: Civil Servant

relatedCareers.reason: Works on analyzing and understanding global political and economic issues.
relatedCareers.title: International Relations Specialist

relatedCareers.reason: Contributes to the development and evaluation of government policies.
relatedCareers.title: Policy Analyst

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, the Indian Foreign Service is actively promoting gender diversity and seeing increasing female representation.
